entry.title: Field for Net value in the Fiori App Sales Volume Flexible Analysis
entry.content:
Hello everyone, I am working with the standard Fiori App Sales Volume Flexible Analysis. I found out that the technical name of the CDS view for this Fiori App is I_BillingDocumentItemCube. There is one field named Net Value in this Fiori App. I was not able to find the corresponding field in the CDS view for Net Value. Can anyone please help me out with this.

@SAPSupport @DominikTylczyn @Mrak1 @fabio_perfetto @Margit_Wagner @AndreasMuno @Dsk @Enda @Christian_Ries @tamas_hoznek @Sandra_Rossi @abo

entry.title: Fiori Overview page - global filter mapping to card filter
entry.content:
Hi,

in global filter i have multiple filter for example : PlantCard1 , PlantCard2.

multiple cards like : Card1 , Card2. pointing to same odata service entity sets field Plant.

for selectionvariant i want to map filter values of PlantCard1 to Plant for Card1 and PlantCard2 to Plant for Card2.

i see in Selectionvariant - there is option for Parameter and Select option - to pass hardcoded value and by default global filter gets mapped if field names are same. but in case we need map global filter A to card filter B there is no option .

anyone encountered this kind of scenario or any solution ? thanks.

entry.title: ECP LDB PNP get pernr returning all records irrespective of data selection period
entry.content:
Hello Team,

I have created a Z program where i am using LBD PNP to get the data for employees in my ECP system. How ever I notice that when i am using GET PERNR it is fetcing all the data irrespective of the data selection period. E.g. I am having 3 records in my IT0000 and in the report when i am passing current date in the data selection period then also it is fetching 3 records instead of fetching the valid record. Can you please let me know why this is happening in ECP. Is there any config change that needs to be done.

Thanks,

Salish

entry.title: I am getting error message while integrating SAP Enable now with SAP On-Premise system .
entry.content:
Hello everyone

When integrating SAP Enable now with the SAP S/4 Hana On-premise system, I am receiving the attached error message.

I have completed all of the SAP GUI's recommended configurations.

Please see what I overlooked and provide guidance.

Thanks 

Manish Gangwal

entry.title: Re: How Do You Decide Your SAP S/4HANA Migration Path?
Enablement of candidate merge jobs 6-7 years after implementation
entry.content:
Hi team,

Our client is using SF recruiting from almost 2017, but not using any candidate profile conversion jobs. Now we want to implement the same, as we have a dependency on our 2 recent projects(eg: OpenText, WOTC)

We have over 2 millions of candidate profiles already in our system. If we go for a complete merge of all the profiles, we foresee 2 different issues

1. Mails will trigger to all the candidates in the system(candidates/employees might get confused of the sudden mail trigger). We can't even enable single sender email as w might lose track of important emails

2. Due to high volume of profiles, the job might take a very long time to run(might break in between)

We are planning to go with 1 week of data for profile conversion initially. 

Does any one have any recommendation on this on how you handled this situation for any other clients?

Regards,

Aswini

entry.title: Re: How to populate compa ratios from the employees profile in reports.
entry.content:
Hi,

We tried breaking our head into this and following is what we did

- Created a custom field with a business rule to have the value from system comp ratio and multiplied it by 1 to manage the business rule. However, this has an issue that it required one time refresh for the comp info portlet for the  value to update for all employees and it does not get updated whenever there is a mass upload or interface input to the SAP EC. Hence we had to stop this custom field 

- We then configured the Story report (taking help from the SAP standard story report set up for Comp ratio). It requires understanding on how the salary range matrix is set up and then called into the table for all fields from job info and foundation master. Then added a rule to report the range where all pay range foundation master matches the job info of the employee. It worked and created a table view data which is downloadable. 

Hope this helps

entry.title: Error Encountered During Status change for Standard Change
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

I encountered an error message when the status changed from "In Development" to "To Be Tested" for the standard change: "The transport-related checks have not passed."

Please provide any guidance.

Regards
Jisna Paul

entry.title: how to solve "Version Indicator is not Activated" for international in during migration of supplier?

Dear Team,

please help to solve below error coming during migration of vendor. How we can check which version indicator maintained here. as in ECC we can use Tcode - SA09. but here in cloud we cant.

Environment -> SAP S/4 HANA Public Cloud Edition 

App -> Migrate Your Data - Migration Cockpit

entry.title: Quick Actions for Joule

Is it mandatory to have Employee Central Quick Action templates configured to use Joule Transactional use cases.

Thanks,

Anand

entry.title: Re: Dimensions Account SAC
entry.content:
@Bernardo_Santos1 

You can only load master data with flat file, if the dimension is a public dimension. So If your Account dimension is a public dimension then you can load it through file.

 

Hope this helps !!

Nikhil

entry.title: SAP Upgradation Error
entry.content:
Dear Expert,

We recently upgraded SAP from version 9.3 to version 10 with the latest PL. During the upgrade, we encountered an error. Following the SAP note, we removed the BP address UDF in version 9.3, and the upgrade was completed successfully.

However, after upgrading the SAP database to version 10 without the UDF, we are now facing an issue. When attempting to recreate the BP address UDF, we are receiving an error stating that the entry already exists.

Could you please advise on how to resolve this issue? We need the UDF as we have important data stored in the deleted UDF.

 
Attached Error.
 
Regards
Krishna Varma

entry.title: Re: How to capture intermediate SSO screen while opening SAP WebGUI using SAP iRPA
entry.content:
We are facing the same issue when trying to login to S4 Public Cloud with an SSO screen in between. Did you already find a solution? For us it seems impossible to get the screen shot to be unique:

Any help is appreciated!

entry.title: Re: The initial value in table structure is not matching...
entry.content:
Hi Manish

 

That's correct. BW providers does not allow NULL values. BW infoprovider tables are always defined with the "NOT NULL" property.

 

br

Roland

entry.title: Re: SAC Deployments via APIs

entry.title: Extract ANLC Asset Value Fields Data in CSV File Using ABAP Program For Selected Cost Center
entry.content:
FIXED ASSET Extract ANLC Asset Value Fields Data in CSV File Using ABAP Program For Selected Cost Center -

------------------------------------

ABAP Program - Z_ANLC_FIXEDASSET 

  • Program Type – ABAP 
  • ABAP Program Name – Z_ANLC_FA 
  • Tcode - ZHM_ANLC_A 
  • Output File Location - “C:\tmp” 
  • Output File Name- ANLC.CSV 
  • Output File Type – CSV (Column with Semicolon separator (;) 
  • SAP Table Name – ANLC 
  • List of fields to be extracted -  
  • ANLN1 
  • KANSW 
  • KNAFA 
  • NAFAP 
  • GJAHR 

-----------------------------------------------------------------------

REPORT  Z_ANLC_FIXEDASSET.
 
TYPE-POOLS: VRM.
 
DATA: NAME  TYPE VRM_ID,
      LIST  TYPE VRM_VALUES,
      VALUE LIKE LINE OF LIST.
 
PARAMETERS: ENTITY(10) AS LISTBOX VISIBLE LENGTH 10.
PARAMETERS : OPATH TYPE STRING DEFAULT '\\MYSERVER\tmp\ANLC.csv'.
 
AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT.
 
NAME = 'ENTITY'.
 
VALUE-KEY = 'UK01'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK01'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.
 
VALUE-KEY = 'UK75'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK75'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.
 
VALUE-KEY = 'UK21'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK21'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.
 
VALUE-KEY = 'UK31'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK31'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.
 
VALUE-KEY = 'UK41'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK41'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.
 
VALUE-KEY = 'UK02'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK02'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.
 
VALUE-KEY = 'UK73'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK73'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.
 
VALUE-KEY = 'UK04'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK04'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.
 
VALUE-KEY = 'UK61'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK61'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.
CALL FUNCTION 'VRM_SET_VALUES' EXPORTING ID = NAME VALUES = LIST.
 
START-OF-SELECTION.
 
type-pools:TRUXS.
data: begin of itab occurs 0,
      anln1 like anlc-anln1,
  kansw like anlc-kansw,
  knafa like anlc-knafa,
   nafap like anlc-nafap,
   gjahr like anlc-gjahr,
   BUKRS like anlc-BUKRS,
      end of itab.
data:  itab1 type TRUXS_T_TEXT_DATA.
 
 select  ANLC~anln1
ANLC~kansw
ANLC~knafa
   ANLC~nafap
   ANLC~gjahr
   ANLC~BUKRS
         from ANLC  INNER JOIN ANLA ON ANLC~ANLN1 = ANLA~ANLN1
         into table itab
         WHERE ANLC~BUKRS = ENTITY AND ANLA~DEAKT IN ('00000000').
 
CALL FUNCTION 'SAP_CONVERT_TO_CSV_FORMAT'
 EXPORTING
   I_FIELD_SEPERATOR          = ','
  TABLES
    I_TAB_SAP_DATA             = itab
 CHANGING
   I_TAB_CONVERTED_DATA       =  itab1
 EXCEPTIONS
   CONVERSION_FAILED          = 1
   OTHERS                     = 2
          .
IF SY-SUBRC <> 0.
 MESSAGE ID SY-MSGID TYPE SY-MSGTY NUMBER SY-MSGNO
         WITH SY-MSGV1 SY-MSGV2 SY-MSGV3 SY-MSGV4.
ENDIF.
 
   CALL FUNCTION 'GUI_DOWNLOAD'
    EXPORTING
      filename = OPATH
    TABLES
      data_tab = itab1
    EXCEPTIONS
      OTHERS   = 1.

entry.title: Re: SAC Deployments via APIs
entry.content: Me too

entry.title: SAP Cloud ALM Analytics - What's new in Q3 2024
entry.content:
SAP Cloud ALM continues to improve and expand its API catalog. In Q3 2024, several enhancements were released in the area of ​​analytics and OpenTelemetry APIs.

Specific OData Analytics APIs

In addition to the generic SAP Cloud ALM analytics OData API and REST API, several specialized analytics OData API are now available:

  • Requirements Analytics OData API
  • Projects Analytics OData API
  • Tasks Analytics OData API
  • Defects Analytics OData API
  • Tests Analytics OData API
  • ConfigurationItems Analytics OData API

The specialized OData APIs are more convenient to use than the generic OData API Dataset since they expose real names for dimensions and measures instead of generic names (dxk/dxv,mxk/mxv). Additionally, strong typing and simplified API usage make it significantly easier to create data models in SAP Analytics Cloud. In few clicks you are ready to build your first story in SAP Analytics Cloud.

Raw Data Inbound Traces API for RUM and SUM

It's now possible to send the OpenTelemetry spans of your non-SAP applications to SAP Cloud ALM Real User Monitoring with the Raw Traces Inbound API. All features of Real User Monitoring become accessible to the non-SAP landscape (Unspecific On-Premise System & Unspecific Cloud Service (HTTP).

With the same API you can also send your non-SAP agent activities to SAP Cloud ALM Synthetic User Monitoring. All features of Synthetic User Monitoring are fully functional.

 

 

Raw Data Outbound Logs API for IEP

SAP Cloud ALM Raw Logs Oubound API now exposes the Monitoring Events of scenario Intelligent Event Processing. Once enabled, 24 hours of history become accessible with OpenTelemetry protocol. More details available in sap help documentation.

Raw Data Inbound Logs API for BSM

You can now send with SAP Cloud ALM Raw Logs Inbound API, OpenTelemetry logs for creation, update, start and closure of status events and visualize your status events with SAP Cloud ALM Business Service Management.

More details available in sap help documentation.

APIs Self-monitoring

Building integration scenarios and pipelines with SAP Cloud ALM APIs, often with 24/7 requirements, means that the APIs themselves must be closely monitored to react quickly to any eventuality.

This is now possible for some APIs (including OpenTelemetry and Analytics APIs) with the help of SAP Cloud ALM Exception Monitoring.

Check the details here.

Raw Data Outbound Metrics API Best Practices

New recommendations are available for those who use SAP Raw Metrics Outbound APIs.

entry.title: Re: SetDimension filter on Level Based Hierarchy
entry.content:
@GST11 

Thanks for the tag. 

For level based hierarchy you should try something like below

 

var DS= Table_1.getDataSource();
DS.setDimensionFilter("FA","[FA].[Level].&[9307]");

//[FA].[Level].&[9307] -  FA is Dimension Name, [Level] is Level based Hier,9307 is the member

 

The filter member should be in the pattern "[FA].[Level].&[9307]" which is as below

[DimName].[Level HierName].&[memberid]

 

Hope this helps !!

Nikhil

entry.title: sap
entry.content: Hi there are existing company codes and plants in ecc. those need to transfer to gts how?

entry.title: Re: CL_SALV_TABLE number of rows
entry.content:
Thank you!

I managed to get the ALV with CF 'GET_GLOBALS_FROM_SLVC_FULLSCR' in the 'after_salv_function' event and get the number I wanted, and display it in the ALV list header with 'set_list_header' method of cl_salv_table display settings.
I don't know if there is a better way to do this, so feel free to  criticize my solution.

(gv_total is is the number of lines in the original table)

 

DATA: go_table  TYPE REF cl_salv_table.

DATA: gv_total  TYPE i,

      gv_totalc TYPE char10.
DATA: lo_events TYPE REF cl_salv_events_table.

lo_events ?= go_table->get_event( ).

SET HANDLER lcl_handle_events=>after_salv_function  FOR lo_events. 
METHOD after_salv_function.



  DATA: lo_grid             TYPE REF TO cl_gui_alv_grid,

        lo_display          TYPE REF TO cl_salv_display_settings,

        lt_filtered_entries TYPE lvc_t_fidx,

        lv_count            TYPE i,

        lv_countc           TYPE char10,

        lv_title            TYPE lvc_title.



  IF lo_grid IS INITIAL.

    CALL FUNCTION 'GET_GLOBALS_FROM_SLVC_FULLSCR'

      IMPORTING

        e_grid = lo_grid.

  ENDIF.



  lo_grid->get_filtered_entries( IMPORTING et_filtered_entries = lt_filtered_entries ).



  DESCRIBE TABLE lt_filtered_entries LINES lv_count.

  lv_countc = gv_total - lv_count.

  CONDENSE lv_countc.

  CONCATENATE lv_countc 'records selected out of' gv_totalc ' total' INTO lv_title SEPARATED BY space.



  lo_display = go_table->get_display_settings( ).

  lo_display->set_list_header( lv_title ).



ENDMETHOD.

entry.title: Re: Enter does not work when I deploy my RAP app in Fiori...
entry.content:
Hi Marian,

Thank you for getting back on this topic.

We have a List report with multiple fields. When a value is entered in Fields A, the value should be automatically updated in Fields B. This usually happens when you press "Enter" when I open the application from the Service Binding preview option. But when I do the same with the Fiori App deployed on the Fiori Launch pad, the enter doesn't work and we have to either refresh the page of press on the search button.

To answer you question, we are on the list field when "Enter" is pressed. 

Please let me know if you need any further details.

Thank you

Best Regards

Pradeep.

entry.title: Best practices for SAP Hana & WMS system integration
entry.content:
Hi,

What are the best practices for integrating SAP HANA with Warehouse Management Systems (WMS), specifically regarding data synchronization methodologies, transactional integrity, performance optimization, and ensuring system scalability and high availability?

Thanks

entry.title: SAC Planning Import API Performance
entry.content:
Hello,

We are using the SAC Import API in our projects in order to integrate our calculations across all of our projects.

Which documented here: SAP Analytics Cloud REST API , It works fine for lots of rows like 100k, 250k but when it comes to 1 or 1k rows, it takes too much time to import them(20 seconds minimum). 

 The sample import process is like following:

  1. Calling the "Create job for model" API /models/{modelID} (POST)
  2. Attaching the import rows with /jobs/{jobID} (POST)
  3. Triggering the import job /jobs/{jobID}/run
  4. Calling the job status API until the job finishes /jobs/{jobID}/status

Is there any way to speed up this process?

Best Regards

entry.title: Re: Ideas for Fiori Tools: More sandbox functionality, global NPM dependencies
entry.content:
One way you could consider addressing the second point is by supporting PNPM out of the box:

Motivation | pnpm

entry.title: SE73, Barcode with type QR CODE 2005 runs in error if Backslash "\" is in Content String
entry.content:
Hey Folks, 

I need that following string in an QR-barcode for an sapscript forms:

[)>\1E06\1D2K7050100200\1DPPPS5903\1D1TRM199\1DQ30

In SE73  we have already an existing Barcode type "QR Code 2005" which works fine, but if i try the String mentioned above the Barcode test in SE73 returns: 

"operation RENDERBARCODE(BARCODE) failed:rendering this barcode failed!"

If i replace in the String above for example all backslash with another character, QR code works fine.
If i test with an online Tool QR Generator - with the string including the backslash above, everything fine, so just SAP does not obviously allow the backslash. 

Unfortenately i need the backslash in the QR Code Content. We use the following settings in SE73:

Mode: A
Module Size: 25
ErrCorrLev (L,M,Q,H): H

According to SAP Note i tried already changing the Mode from A to all other possible entries, no change in behaviour. 

Any ideas?

BR Gregor

entry.title: Re: Ideas for Fiori Tools: More sandbox functionality, global NPM dependencies
entry.content: One way you could consider addressing the second point is by supporting PNPM out of the box: https://pnpm.io/motivation

  • no explanation: check

entry.title: Revolutionizing Support feature of SAP PaPM Cloud UM with Built-In Support
entry.content:
In today’s fast-paced business environment, providing a seamless support experience is crucial for improving customer satisfaction. SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud - Universal Model (SAP Cloud - UM) has already introduced with SAP Companion to users. you can check out the created by my colleague, @KatrinaMendoza , for the Introduction to SAP Companion. In our continuous efforts to improve, SAP PaPM Cloud - UM is now offering an integrated, modernized support experience with SAP Built-In Support.

SAP Built-In Support was developed with the goal to support users within the SAP Cloud solution they are working with by providing user with sufficient information so that creating an incident may be avoided. By integrating a direct access to tools like SAP Documentation and ServiceNow, users can quickly find references, get recommendation, and can request support, improving efficiency and enhancing the overall customer experience.

What I would like to share with you in this blogpost are the following

1. How to access SAP Built-In Support
2. When to use Built-In Support

Ready for some excitement? Let's dive right in and get started!

1.) How to access SAP Built-In Support. 
To begin using SAP Built-In Support, Click the Support Icon:  Look for the support icon or button upper right side of the application .

      

2.) When to use SAP Built-In Support

Imagine you're working on a SAP PaPM Cloud UM model and encounter a situation where you're unsure how a specific function works or need more information about its behavior. Here’s how you can make use of the built-in support features:

Built-In Support for SAP Documentation

At the top of the interface, you'll find the Search Support Knowledge box, which provides direct access to product support knowledge content. This search-as-you-type feature offers suggestions considering the SAP PaPM Cloud - UM Application Help, making it easy to find relevant information.

On the launchpad or home page, you'll also see recommendations from the SAP PaPM Community, including links to the main page and the What’s New blog for Universal Model updates.

Additionally, the built-in support system provides tailored recommendations based on the application or tiles you are currently in, ensuring that you receive the most relevant information for your needs 

How cool,    right ?  

Though, Let’s say after all the searching and checking the recommendation provided and still not able to find answers or probably experience a technical issue or a bug, it was made easy for the user to log an incident without even going to SAP ServiceNow. Below is how the user can report and view an incident.

Built-In Support for Incident Reporting
The procedure below will go through the process of creating an incident report using the Built-In Support process.

Pre-requisite: S-USER to access SAP’s Support Portal

1. Open the Built-In support clicking its icon in top right corner of the application and sign in using SAP universal ID, which has an authorized S-User link.     

2. Back in the Built-In Support dashboard and two tiles will be available,
    "Get Support" and "View Cases".

    

    To create an Incident Report

2.1 Choose Get Support and fill-out the required fields (*). Key in the necessary information as shown in a sample below.



A. Subject: Title of the issue

B. Description: (Provide the following information)

Detailed explanation of what happened (include steps to reproduce the problem)
Expected Outcome and Actual Result
Mention the Logs about the error        

C. Component: SAP component related to issue in this case use LOD-PER-ACE

D. Priority: Choose a priority level based on SAP Note 67739 which you can find also by clicking  beside  Priority.

E. Attachment: Attach any supporting documents, logs, or screenshots that can help SAP understand the issue better. This could include system error logs or examples of the problem.

             Note:  This does not replace the Incident Logging process but provide an alternative way.

2.2 Choose Create Case

          To view  and reply to an Incident Report

2.3 Choose View Support and as shown below.   

            

The user will be able to reply to and update cases as well when necessary. 


                                   
The user will also be able to see the replies from SAP.    
        

SAP Built-In Support is here to simplify your experience and ensure you have the information you need at your fingertips. Whether you’re searching for documentation, seeking community recommendations, or reporting incidents, this support system enhances your efficiency and helps you overcome challenges.

That’s it! Embrace the power of SAP PaPM Cloud UM Built-In Support to maximize your productivity! See you in my next blog!

entry.title: Re: Sybase IQ 16.1 OLEDB driver problem with SQL Server I...
entry.content: Hello Roland, today we had call with MS support and problem is in OLEDB32.DLL, that is changed after mid 2023 OS patching. I hope that workaround provided by support will close this issue.

entry.title: Re: From tech sales to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public
entry.content: Thank you kindly Cristiano Pinto!

entry.title: The Procurement Monthly - October 24
entry.content:
This is a blog by Gordon Donovan

 

Summary

Risk, cost resilience, Data, AI and digital. These are the main themes that emerge from this months reports. Procurement gravitating to much more of a risk based function. Organisations everywhere wanting to at least pilot AI use cases, mainly to concentrate more time on risk and complexity, and whilst inflation may be coming down, worries of recension, geopolitical uncertainty are still dominating agendas.

 

Procurement Leaders – Scope 3

This study of the PL membership looks at how procurement functions are measuring and managing the scope 3 emissions and some of the potential problems they are facing.

 We had suppliers that were switching from coal in some of their factories, but in a factory that doesn't make our product. Their overall emissions reduced greatly because of the switch, but it didn't help my product at all

The paper suggest 4 steps to help tackle the challenge.

  • Build the capability to progress data quality
  • Partner internally to incorporate data into the process
  • Segment suppliers and tailor engagement methods
  • Explore working with competitors to lighted the load

As you can see from the 4 steps, one of the biggest challenges on scope 3 is the accuracy (and relevance) of the data with the paper stating that 33% identified this as the main barrier for calculating scope 3 emissions.

The report also identifies that you cant impact Scope 3 without your suppliers (as its them who are creating the emission!

Currently, the top engagement method is emails with 87.5%, with training 75% and events and policy just over 50%. Only 5% of respondents said suppliers were not engaged which is a good outcome.

 

AQPC Procurement – Procurement Blue Print

This pretty comprehensive (74 page) book covers a range of benchmarks and findings from the various surveys that they carry out.

Some interesting stats total cost to perform procurement (this is P2P) is 2.80 per $1000 in revenue. Cycle times 1 day for issuing goods  or services PO. 90% of PO approved electronically,1473 PO processed per FTE

There are also some data points for supplier management, risk management and supply chain visibility that are worth while diving into as is the SAP Benchmarking report as well for comparison.

 

CIPS Outlook

One of the biggest impacts this year has been the risk of inflation, and of the amount of political events that could impact both inflation and potential recessions. The chief economist for Chartered Institute of Procurement & Supply (CIPS) reviews a quarterly pulse survey from (mainly) UK and Europe CPO’s.

Key takeaways are that headline risk is declining (slowly) from a very high point (4.53 to 4.02) in the last 6 months. Geopolitical factors continue to be the primary drivers of experience shortages in supply chains. With over 70% of experience shortages being attributed to this factor. Labour shortages continue to drive 43% of experience shortages in supply chains, which reflects high levels of employment in many economies.

Inflationary pressures in the global economy have abated. Inflation is moving into or close to central bank targets in Europe, the US and throughout the rest the world.  From the supply chain perspective it is unlikely that the disruption in the Red Sea is likely to be resolved any time soon. As a result, sea cargo will continue to transit around the Cape, increasing both transit times and cost. It is therefore not unsurprising that where procurement costs have increased by more than 10%, one-in-five of the drivers of these cost increases have been associated with logistics costs.

Given the levels of risk still existing in global supply chains, the key strategies that procurement managers and supply chain professionals are going to deploy to mitigate risk have not changed. Specifically, supply chain managers will continue to try and diversify the number of suppliers, extend contracts and hold more stocks. These three strategies have been the top ranked strategies in all three pulse studies conducted in 2024.

 

Oliver Wyman Procurement Study

In this survey, the authors sought detailed perspective on the most important issues for procurement professionals and the tactics they’re employing to tackle them effectively.

From a sourcing perspectives these priorities were unlocking supplier relationship value, mitigating inflation, and adjustment and refining of processes and strategies.  It wouldn’t be a survey unless it includes some aspect of AI!

In this study, the barriers for adoption were the lack of expertise, and integration with existing systems, followed by data quality and (interestingly) resistance to change from within procurement teams. Contract management, planning and renegotiation led the functionality of AI being implemented or considered.

 

Procurecon European CPO Study

Another survey this time from Procurecon, focusing on European CPOs was released this month.

The research indicates the CPO’s influence on C-suite decision-making has increased in 91% of organisations. Notably, no respondents reported a decrease. This follows the The Economist report of the last couple of years so its good to see this consistency.

Apparently AI is a thing! It features prominently in CPOs’ strategies moving forward. To free up resources and focus on ‘higher-level’ targets. The survey reveals 49% of respondents have already implemented AI solutions into select processes.

Echoing the CIPS report earlier, the report stated that Supply chain disruptions were cited as the most significant challenge by 26% of respondents, and inflation was the most challenging for 19%.

“I think automating simple tasks is the area that AI can bring the biggest value to procurement, so that you can focus resources on the priority tasks”

There is an interesting section around AI, both with a priorities and also a how to measure which I think is really interesting as procurement seeks to measure the value it brings beyond savings alone.

The areas for AI that  respondents are prioritising supplier risk management (54%), strategic sourcing & supplier selection (50%) and contract negotiation & management (47%) with measures including increased efficiency , better/faster decisions and improved supplier relationships leading the way. I was surprised to see cost savings low at 28% (as a benefit of AI) bearing in mind sourcing was so high as an area where it will be deployed however.

The demand for cost management persists (and in my opinion always will be) According to the research, spend analysis and category management (40%), supplier performance management (40%), and technology for automation and data analysis (38%) are the most effective methods deployed for managing costs within organisations.

 

EY Supply Chain Influence

This supply chain survey about supply chain influence was released this month from EY.

Modernizing the supply chain through technology and digitalization is one of the most pressing challenges for supply chain leaders in the past year. Leaders overall consider supply chain modernization through tech/digital a key priority today. There is however some disaconnects between C suite and leaders. There is a notable gap between the expectations of C-suite and supply chain executives regarding digital maturity within the organization and the timeline for their organizations’ supply chains to become mostly autonomous, with the C-suite more conservative and skeptical compared with supply chain executives:

  • The C-suite (26%) say that their organizations’ digital connectivity with suppliers is limited to email and sharing spreadsheets (vs. 16% of supply chain executives).
  • The C-suite (25%) are less likely to believe their organizations’ supply chains will be mostly autonomous by 2030 (vs. 39% of supply chain executives).

Risk management and revenue growth, supply chain resilience and agility have become paramount. Resiliency is firmly established as a top supply chain objective, with 87% of supply chain leaders overall saying their organizations have made significant investments to improve it. Yet, 24% of supply chain leaders report that their organization is not very or at all prepared for a pandemic or widespread health crisis, and 19% are similarly unprepared for supply shortages, signalling the need for further enhancement of supply chain robustness.

 

Supplier.IO State of Supplier Diversity

The 7th iteration of this report was released this month and the awareness and reporting to the exec level is increasing with 56% now saying that these results make it to the board (2x increase from 2021).

Key drivers are also changing with notably one of the new ones this year being the ability to win new business for their organisations (48%), which really does help procurement with the business value and contribution to growth targets that many now have and its notable that customer requirements as a key driver has increased as has compliance.

Total spend is still the standard metric  with impact, (both for your business and an economic impact of the program being key measurables as well.

Tracking suppliers leans much more toward 3rd party accreditation rather than self-certified being included in the data.

 

Gartner – Supply Chain Technology Trends

This article is based on a a few studies that Gartner has carried out over recent periods. 

The top trends this year have two broad themes: “control and protect” and “humans and machines.” These trends are interconnected, and their importance differs not only by organizational maturity but also by industry, business needs and previously devised strategic plans. Understanding the business strategy and deciding how procurement will support it are critical for identifying which capabilities need to be enabled or matured with technology. The No. 1 motivating factor for investing in supply chain technology for organizations is the need to make the decision-making process faster, more intelligent and of higher quality.

I also found it interesting that supporting new operating models was #2. This supports research from the Economist about the direction of travel of operating models leading to differing models, and well what I can I say about trading partner networks!

 

Art of Procurement – Orchestration

This report from Art of Procurement & Oro looks at how tasks are currently and potentially orchestrated in the future. By understanding how everything connects can procurement design a user experience that supports what the business needs.

Orchestration is enabling and empowering people and tools. It’s all about making their lives easier with the user experience.

Some interesting takeaways. 53% identified spreadsheets as  a wat to coordinate processes, and 48% said email. These two whilst useful still contribute far too much in procurement, which as a results slows down and makes at least semi manual most processes. Which is reflected in the stat that only 2.9% have completed automated procurement processes, with nearly 38% being 50/50.

With all of that, I’m not surprised that around 40% do not agree that procurement is easy to work with. (in fact I’m surprised its not higher)!

 

IDC Worldwide Procurement

One of a few reports released by IDC this month. Two notable takeaways for me, Users identified risk management (14%), the sustainment of cost savings (13%)  and spend data analytics (10%)  as the top three functions on which they spend their time. Risk management rose to the top identified function, again reflective of supply chain challenges.

I mentioned spreadsheets earlier and this report also identifies the top functions used by procurement, with analytics and spend data at 40%, spend visibility at 35% and supplier data at 31%. If procurement is to truly concentrate on adding value, it must digitise these processes to both get better insights, more time and single views ASAP.

As always reach out to discuss more, and always happy to hear your thoughts!

entry.title: Logic for schedule is frozen
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I have a one requirement such that A field indicating when the schedule is frozen should be added to the table (Power BI) which in turn data pulling from CDS View . (It would be worthwhile to just show the status when the schedule is frozen).

So in CDS view what logic have to be written for displaying as schedule is frozen like how can i display schedule is frozen for the Maintenance order or work orders?

below is the standard CDS view C_RSHMaintSchedFrozenOp .we will be creating a customizing cds view on top of this there i need a scneario how can i write a logic to be written for schedule is frozen?

 

Thanks in advance,

entry.title: Re: Custom CDS view : sum
entry.content:
Hi  @chris_SAP 
if i correct understand your question, you can use the following code for CDS:

define view entity Z_YOUR_CDS
  as select from I_PurchaseOrderItemAPI01
{
  key PurchaseOrder,
      count( distinct PurchaseOrderItem ) as CountPurchaseOrderItem
}
group by
  PurchaseOrder

BR,
Oleg

entry.title: OAuth 2.0 client to access external service provider resource
entry.content:
Hello experts,

I am trying to implement an OAuth2.0 connection between SAP and an external service provider. I went through all relevant material ranging from creating a profile through to configuring a client (https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_NETWEAVER_750/3c4e8fc004cb4401a4fdd737f02ac2b9/81c09e3f183e49e4b1eb6959ccbe6e84.html?version=7.5.6 and sub-sections). Then I have a test ABAP program to test the configuration and get an authorization code using the authorization endpoint; after that, I want to call the access token endpoint to get the access token and start calling the resources I want using the obtained access token. The external service provider requires an Authorization Code grant type to be obtained before using it to invoke the access token endpoint.

I have the following questions:

1) according to https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_NETWEAVER_750/3c4e8fc004cb4401a4fdd737f02ac2b9/d7b8d91ad2ef40e6aee5fb071bb75193.html?version=7.5.6, I create an http client but the URL is the target endpoint of the requested resource?

2) when I use the set_token method of the OAuth2.0 client instance - created using the client profile and client configuration - I get the following exception message:

"No access token available for current user."

I attach screenshots from the configuration of the client (transaction OA2C_CONFIG):

The following part of the configuration screen has pre-filled value in the placeholder "Target Endpoint" and it is editable. Do I have to modify it or leave it as it is? if I have to modify it, what value from the service provider should I specify?

Also, the Redirection URI is non-editable and is made up of the Redirection URI server and other stuff.

Does anyone have any example implementations of such a scenario where we want SAP to consume protected resource in an external service provider? 

Thank you in advance,

Konstantinos

entry.title: Re: Custom CDS view : sum
entry.content: I'm on Public Cloud and I can only work with Custom CDS tile

entry.title: Request to Open Source SAP HANA ngdbc JDBC Driver
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

I’m currently working with Trino and am in need of accessing SAP HANA from it. To facilitate this, I’m planning to develop a SAP HANA connector for Trino and contribute it back to the community. However, I’ve encountered a challenge regarding the licensing of the SAP HANA ngdbc JDBC driver.

Since Trino is licensed under the Apache License 2.0, the current license of the ngdbc JDBC driver prevents it from being used as a dependency in this open-source project. I noticed that SAP has previously open-sourced the ODBC driver in the Python library, and I believe it would be incredibly beneficial if the ngdbc JDBC driver could also be open-sourced.

By open-sourcing the ngdbc JDBC driver and making it available in Maven, it would enable wider accessibility to SAP HANA, fostering integration with more platforms like Trino. This would make SAP HANA more accessible to developers working with open-source projects.

Here is the current listing for the ngdbc JDBC driver on Maven:
https://mvnrepository.com/artifact/com.sap.cloud.db.jdbc/ngdbc

I would greatly appreciate your consideration in making this open-source. It would be a fantastic step towards enhancing SAP HANA’s accessibility in the open-source ecosystem.

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: I can not access the OData service from BAS.
entry.content:
check your ui5.yaml file,  do you have something like this?

server:
customMiddleware:
- name: fiori-tools-proxy
afterMiddleware: compression
configuration:
ignoreCertError: true
backend:
- path: /sap
url: "https://******"

entry.title: Re: ECP LDB PNP get pernr returning all records irrespect...
entry.content:
Did you use some of the HR provided macro such as RP_PROVIDE_FROM_FRST or RP_PROVIDE_FROM_LAST ?

 

RP_PROVIDE_FROM_FRST P0001 SPACE RAN_DATE-LOW RAN_DATE-HIGH.

 

 Read Programming Utilities for the Logical Databases PNP and PAP

entry.title: Azure Databricks Connection using SAP PO
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

Does anyone have experience on integrating Azure Databricks with S4 using SAP PO/PI?

We have requirement to pull the delta data from Azure databricks Database to SAP S4 system. We also have SAP PO 7.5 single stack in our landscape for this purpose.. SAP S4 system is expecting the data to be transformed in to an IDOC structure.. So, the question is what would be state of art technology to implement this interface? Should we ask Azure Databricks to execute Query statement to fetch the data from various tables and store it on Azure Databricks temporary storage somewhere and if it is possible to store the data on Azure databricks on some temporary storage? if yes, what should be that temporary storage option available on Azure Databricks side? 

1. SAP PO fetches the data from Azure Databricks and then it transforms the data to IDOC & post it to S4?

(or)

2. SAP PO directly polls the database tables using simple query statement? 

Which approach do you think is the best approach?

Thanks,

Ramu.

entry.title: Re: Executive Review Based on Route Map Step - Release 20...
entry.content: Hi Xavier, sorry it was due to a misunderstanding on my side. I thought the RBP based on Route Step are connected to the Field Based Permission groups. I have created the RBP roles now and everything is working as expected. Thanks for your swift reply and the video!

entry.title: Re: CL_SALV_TABLE number of rows
entry.content:
It's the shortest solution of all.

No need of "IF lo_grid IS INITIAL" you have defined lo_grid as local so the condition is always true. Even if you declare it global, there's almost no performance gain, so keep the code clean without IF and with lo_grid local.

If you use ABAP >= 7.02, use lv_title = |{ lines( alv_table ) - lines( lv_filtered_entries ) } records selected out of { lines( alv_table ) } total| and remove all these unclear and useless global variables. You may even get rid of lv_title and lo_display (no DATA line at all, except lo_grid).

entry.title: Re: ECP LDB PNP get pernr returning all records irrespect...
entry.content: Hello,

entry.title: Intercompany sales during SAP Hana S/4 migration
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

Our company is in the midst of migration to SAP Hana S/4 from ECC 6.0

We are operating in all European countries and  the migration is planned in phases/waves. Current scenario we have multiple company codes and plants in all EU locations running on a single instance of ECC 6

so my question is if we are selling from say Belgium with the delivering plant in France( current scenario) and in the first phase only Belgium CoCode will be migrated to S/4, how do we manages sales with the delivering plant being in France( on old ECC system) not being visible to Belgium Sales Area ( on the new S/4 system)?

This is only one scenario and the reverse situation will also occur with sales from France Sales org( on ECC) being delivered from Belgium plant ( on S/4) 

So we will have two system running in parallel during transition, with no stock visibility between each other, how can this be managed ? 

Please if anyone has been in this situation can share how it was managed ?

Thank you for your responses

Kind Regards

Apoorva

entry.title: REST API with JSON format in SAP BODS as a source
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I'm trying to use REST API as a source in SAP BODS and the response is only JSON format.

I created a WADL file and imported function in DS but getting an error while executing the Job.

Error Details: [JSNONParser]Missing value at line:<1> at column: <20>.

Please let me know solution for this and let me know if any sample reference code for REST API with JSON format as a source in SAP BODS.

Thanks,

Balu

entry.title: Re: What’s New with MDG QuickStart – Richer content , faster projects
entry.content: Hi. Is there a demo for the standard delivered content for the domains included in the Quick Start service (like a click-through demo supported by a scope definition showing the key data elements, activities/workflows etc)? This would be useful to evaluate how closely the standard content fits our requirement to understand the degree of effort required to build out the solution once the quick start content has been activated.

entry.title: Release Navigator for SAP Supply Chain Management solutions - What's New in 2411
entry.content:
Readiness@Scale is excited to support the next major batch of releases for SAP Supply Chain Management solutions for our SAP Enterprise Support customers, increasing innovation adoption within the cloud. The release navigator for SAP Supply Chain Management solutions helps you plan and prepare your system updates, ensuring you have full visibility of release information and enablement. Through the week of November 15th, content & information will be updated within the tool.

 In this blog post, we will cover:

  1. New & Updated Content
  2. Updated innovation & content update plan
  3. How to connect with Readiness@Scale

Here are the highlights of new & updated content within the release navigator:

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Find the 2025 update plan for the release navigator below:

 

 

 

Disclaimer: These are only informational dates that could change. The Readiness@Scale team has the final decision over any UX modifications.

If you have feedback or specific requests of what content you would like to see featured in the release navigator, please do not hesitate to let us know! Use our feedback survey or reply to this blog post in the Comments section below. 

You can learn more about how the Readiness@Scale is a cross-solution team responsible for release readiness topics in the SAP Enterprise Support portfolio at our Release Ready page. Find all our teams blogs with the tag Readiness@Scale.

 

Best Run,

Readiness@Scale

SAP Enterprise Support

entry.title: Re: Copy Measures across hierarchies member using Advance...
entry.content: Thanks this simplify a lot my code, unfortunately no output changes. I got the values from Flow = # to Flow != #, but it copy values to ALL the Flow != #. The deal here is to write only in Flow present in the combination of each row. example from the above tables: The combo of account "C010101", Product "TEST", Plant "PP01" Entity "ENT01" and Owner = "SPA" has three different Flow value: #, 01.01, 55.01. Other combo have different Flow valorized... so I can't proceed with writing values into the totality of Flows (hundreds) for every combination. Hope this can be helpful. Again, thanks for your support.

entry.title: Re: I can not access the OData service from BAS.
entry.content:
server:

   customMiddleware:

     - name: fiori-tools-proxy

      afterMiddleware: compression

         configuration:

                ignoreCertError: true

               ui5:

                path:

            - /resources

             - /test-resources

            url: https://ui5.sap.com

entry.title: Re: Hi I am new to sap build process automation. How to e...
entry.content:
Hi @phaniik 
I recommend to access our Creating Processes and Automations with SAP Build Process Automation 
Where we introduce you to all the important aspects of SAP Build Process Automation, from reusing existing content to building your own automations and processes.
After completing the lesson Leveraging SAP Build Process Automation APIs, you will be able to leverage the SAP Build Process Automation Application Programming Interfaces.

Please also access our SAP Help Portal where you can find details Using SAP Build Process Automation APIs 

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: Scheluding exam problem
entry.content:
Hi @donghwa 

In order to provide individual support please create a ticket in our User Support Center
To help the support team to understand your query better, could you please provide the following information in your correspondence?

  •            Detailed description of the issue
  •            Screenshots: To help us visualize the issue
  •            Please also provide the Course Code of course.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hello @David_Chaviano,

I am very interested in participating in the digital skills initiative. I have not been successful in my current career and am looking to transition to the SAP field as SAP Basis. However, I have limited financial resources, which makes it impossible for me to afford a certification at this time, making this opportunity extremely important to me. I am eager to learn and willing to put in the effort to succeed in an SAP career.

I believe I meet the following criteria:

Minorities who may not have equal opportunities in their geographic location.

People looking to switch their careers mid-life.

People who lack the financial means to afford a certification attempt for themselves.


Thank you in advance for your consideration!

entry.title: Re: A Simplified Guide to Effectively Use the Clearing API
entry.content:
I have payment 000001, with amount 800000
And I used the Journal Entry Clearing api to clear the invoice I0001 with amount 30000 as the partial payment as below. It works well and there is also the clear JE result. 
 
<APARItems>
<AccountingDocument>000001</AccountingDocument>
<PartialPaymentAmtInDspCrcy currencyCode="JPY">300000.00</PartialPaymentAmtInDspCrcy>
 </APARItems>
 <APARItems>
    <AccountingDocument>I0001</AccountingDocument>
 </APARItems>
 
And for the next time, I used the same api and clear the remaining payment amount as below. 
As payment 000001 has made clearing and the amount will be left only 50000. And the invoice I0002 has amount 70000. So, I write the xml logic as below.
 
<APARItems>
<AccountingDocument>000001</AccountingDocument>
</APARItems>
 <APARItems>
    <AccountingDocument>I0002</AccountingDocument>
<PartialPaymentAmtInDspCrcy currencyCode="JPY">500000.00</PartialPaymentAmtInDspCrcy>
</APARItems>
 
I got the error as "Balance in transaction currency Balance in transaction currency JPY 300000- is too large for automatic correction is too large for automatic correction". 
Could you pls guide me how to solve that error?

entry.title: Re: Setup email to actually send to my e-mail address
entry.content: For some testing purpose

entry.title: Crystal Reports for Visual Studio not showing control items in toolbox
entry.content:
After installing CR for VS in VS 2015 (done as Administrator), we can now add a report. However, when going to the toolbox, only four controls are offered.  These are pointer, text, line, and box objects under Crystal Reports. The General category is empty.

When I go to choose items, I see the attached Crystal assemblies already selected.  I noticed that some of the references were still pointing to old assemblies. So, I removed the references, added new, and re-launched the choose items dialogue. I selected the assemblies and STILL no visible control, although there were messages saying some controls, and these were not ones I typically use, having been added but visible or available.

The solution is configured for .NET 3.5, which I know is low.

If anyone can explain to me why I'm not seeing the controls for the designer, I would really appreciate it.  Thank you.

- N

entry.title: Re: I can not access the OData service from BAS.
entry.content: you miss this part backend: - path: /sap url: "https://******"

entry.title: Re: Create data using Journal Entry – Clearing (Asynchron...
entry.content: I have payment 000001, with amount 800000 And I used the Journal Entry Clearing api to clear the invoice I0001 with amount 30000 as the partial payment as below. It works well and there is also the clear JE result. <APARItems> <AccountingDocument>000001</AccountingDocument> <PartialPaymentAmtInDspCrcy currencyCode="JPY">300000.00</PartialPaymentAmtInDspCrcy> </APARItems> <APARItems> <AccountingDocument>I0001</AccountingDocument> </APARItems> And for the next time, I used the same api and clear the remaining payment amount as below. As payment 000001 has made clearing and the amount will be left only 50000. And the invoice I0002 has amount 70000. So, I write the xml logic as below. <APARItems> <AccountingDocument>000001</AccountingDocument> </APARItems> <APARItems> <AccountingDocument>I0002</AccountingDocument> <PartialPaymentAmtInDspCrcy currencyCode="JPY">500000.00</PartialPaymentAmtInDspCrcy> </APARItems> I got the error as "Balance in transaction currency Balance in transaction currency JPY 300000- is too large for automatic correction is too large for automatic correction". Could you pls guide me how to solve that error?
